prerequisites: A Vitally account with access to account settings and the Integrations section (select your Account Logo at the top left, then Operations, then Integrations).
The Vitally REST API integration enabled: in Integrations choose Vitally REST API to reach the REST API settings screen.
An API key (called the secret token in the Vitally UI) created on the REST API settings screen, which is used as the HTTP Basic auth username.
A value for the required domain config field that sets the host requests are sent to.
A value for the required status config field.

authMethods.name: API key (HTTP Basic auth)
authMethods.summary: Vitally authenticates with an API key, called the secret token in the UI, sent via HTTP Basic auth with the key as the username and an empty password (the documented header is Authorization: Basic with the base64 of secret_token followed by a colon). Supply it through the secret_token config field. The REST API settings screen also exposes a ready-made Basic Auth header you can copy into the basic_auth_header secret field instead of having the connector build it from the token.

setupSteps.title: Enable the Vitally REST API and create a key
setupSteps.body: In Vitally, select your Account Logo at the top left, go to Operations then Integrations, and choose Vitally REST API. On the REST API settings screen, create a key. Each key shows an editable name, a copyable secret token, and a copyable Basic Auth header, with options to regenerate or revoke it.

setupSteps.title: Provide the credential
setupSteps.body: Copy the secret token from the REST API settings screen into the secret_token config field. Alternatively, copy the ready-made Basic Auth header into the basic_auth_header field; this is the same credential as the precomputed Authorization: Basic header value.

setupSteps.title: Set the domain
setupSteps.body: Enter the host value in the required domain config field so requests are sent to the correct host.

setupSteps.title: Set the status value
setupSteps.body: Provide a value for the required status config field.
